results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 Book 2 - Lesson 1: 先週、日本に来たばかりです
feedbackText: ❌ Not quite. The correct answer is ホイアンという町まち. Use という to introduce a name the listener may not know. Option A uses の which doesn't have the "called" nuance, and option C uses は which changes the sentence structure incorrectly.
❌ Not quite. The correct answer is という. The pattern is: Name + は「meaning」+ という + 意味です.
✅ Correct! ナンというパンです — "It's a bread called naan." N1 という N2 directly connects the name to the category.
❌ Not quite. The correct answer is 来たばかりです (きたばかりです). Use the た-form of 来る (来た) + ばかりです.

❌ Not quite. The correct answer is 習い始めたばかりです. Remember: ばかりです attaches to the た-form (past tense) of the verb, not dictionary or て-form. Option A uses dictionary form (始める) and C has a conjugation error (ばかります doesn't exist).

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 Book 2 - Lesson 10: どんな服を着て行けばいいですか？
feedbackText: ❌ Not quite. The answer is ばいいですか. 持って行く → 持って行けば (ば-form of 行く: -ku → -ke + ば) + いいですか.
❌ Not quite. Group 2 ば-form: drop る, add れば. 着る → 着れば. えば is for Group 1 verbs.
❌ Not quite. ばいいですか = general custom/rule inquiry. たらいいですか = personal advice. They overlap, but ば feels more "what's the proper way?" while たら feels more "what do you suggest for me?"
❌ Not quite. てはいけません = formal prohibition (rules, signs). ちゃだめ = casual (friends, daily speech). Same meaning, different register.
❌ Not quite. The answer is なくちゃ. 片付ける → 片付けない (nai form) → 片付けなくちゃ (casual obligation).

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 Book 2 - Lesson 11: ポイントカードを忘れてしまいました
feedbackText: ❌ Not quite. The answer is みてもいいですか. 着る → 着て (te-form) + みてもいいですか (may I try?).
❌ Not quite. V-てみてもいいですか combines V-てみる (try doing) and V-てもいいですか (permission). V-てある is a state result — different pattern entirely.
❌ Not quite. みてもいいですか asks for permission to try. みますか just asks "will you try?" — missing the permission element that's essential in a store setting.
❌ Not quite. The answer is すぎます. ナ-adj: drop な, add すぎます directly. 派手（な）→ 派手すぎます.
❌ Not quite. イ-adjectives keep い before の: 大きいの. 大きな is a special pre-noun modifier (大きな犬) — different usage from the pronoun の pattern.

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 Book 2 - Lesson 12: この掃除機は軽くて動かしやすいですよ
feedbackText: ❌ Not quite. The answer is まで. V-る (dictionary form) + まで = "until [action happens]." 届く is already in dictionary form, so just add まで.
❌ Not quite. Dictionary form + まで. て-form + まで doesn't work. If you want "after doing," that's V-てから (MNN L16), a different pattern entirely.
❌ Not quite. V-るまで uses dictionary form for the endpoint action. 来た is past tense — you can't say "until came" when the action hasn't happened yet. Use 来る (dictionary) + まで.
❌ Not quite. The answer is 使いにくい. 使います → stem: 使い + にくい. にくい = "hard to ~." The vacuum is heavy and hard to use — not a great product!
❌ Not quite. ます-stem: remove ます from the polite form. 使います → 使い + やすい/にくい. This is the same stem used for V-ます, V-たい, V-に行く, etc.

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 Book 2 - Lesson 13: いろいろな資料を展示してあります
feedbackText: ❌ Not quite. The answer is 建てられました. 建てる → 建てられる (Group 2 passive: drop る, add られる). In passive ④, the created object is the subject.
❌ Not quite. によって = "by [creator]." It marks who made, built, or created something. It's distinct from に (which marks the agent in person-direct passives like L10).
❌ Not quite. Transitive verbs (他動詞) imply someone's intention: 開ける→開けてある. Intransitive verbs (自動詞) describe states without implying who did it: 開く→開いている.
❌ Not quite. つけてあります uses transitive つける + てある = "turned on (on purpose)." Intransitive つく pairs with ている: ついている = "the lights are on" (neutral state).
❌ Not quite. The answer is だけじゃなくて. N1 + だけじゃなくて = "not only N1." Then N2 + も = "N2 also." Together: "not only N1, but also N2."

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 Book 2 - Lesson 14: 前髪は、もう少し短く切ってもらえますか？
feedbackText: ❌ Not quite. The answer is 短く. イ-adjective adverb form: 短い → 短く. The く form modifies the verb 切る, describing how to cut.
❌ Not quite. ナ-adj uses に, not く. きれいに (tidily), 静かに (quietly). The く conversion is exclusively for イ-adjectives.
❌ Not quite. The answer is きれいに. きれい is a ナ-adj, so: な → に. きれいに + 掃除する = "clean tidily."
❌ Not quite. 短くする is correct. You must convert い→く before attaching to する. The adverb form is required whenever an adjective modifies a verb.
❌ Not quite. Correct! 短く犬 is wrong — you can't modify nouns with the adverb form. Nouns need the adjective form: 短い犬. The く/に form is exclusively for modifying verbs.

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 Book 2 - Lesson 15: 会議室の電気がついたままでした
feedbackText: ❌ Not quite. The answer is たまま. つく → ついた (た-form) + まま. V-たまま describes a state that persists unchanged.
❌ Not quite. た-form, not て-form. The past tense form describes the action whose result persists: ついた (turned on → still on), 開いた (opened → still open).
❌ Not quite. たまま = state persists unchanged. ながら = simultaneous actions (eating while watching TV). Here, the AC wasn't actively being turned on while sleeping — it was left on.
❌ Not quite. そのまま = "as is / leave it unchanged." It's the pronoun version of まま. Very useful when someone offers to tidy up and you want them to stop!
❌ Not quite. The answer is たまま. 開く → 開いた (た-form) + まま. Another common eco-warning: windows left open wastes heating/cooling energy.

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 Book 2 - Lesson 16: 地震が来ても、あわてて動かないでください
feedbackText: ❌ Not quite. Group 2: る → ろ. 逃げる → 逃げろ. The れ ending is for Group 1 verbs (行く → 行け).
❌ Not quite. The answer is 走るな. Prohibition = dictionary form + な. Same for all groups: 走る+な, 見る+な, する+な, 来る+な.
❌ Not quite. ないようにして adds "try to / make effort to" — a softer, more considerate request. ないで is a direct prohibition. Different nuance, not just different politeness level.
❌ Not quite. The answer is ないようにしてください. 吸う → 吸わない + ようにしてください = "please try not to ~."
❌ Not quite. 開く (intransitive: "the door opens by itself") → 開かない + なくなる = "stops opening." 開ける (transitive: "someone opens") would describe a person's ability, not the door's state.

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 Book 2 - Lesson 17: 日本語が前より話せるようになりました
feedbackText: ❌ Not quite. The answer is 話せるようになりました. 話す → 話せる (potential) + ようになりました. This expresses gradual growth from inability to ability.
❌ Not quite. わかる and できる are inherently potential verbs — they already express ability. Adding another potential form would be redundant and incorrect. Just use them directly + ようになりました.
❌ Not quite. This pattern specifically marks a change — something you could NOT do before, and NOW you can. The なりました (became) is what makes it a change expression.
❌ Not quite. The answer is 楽しめるようになりました. 楽しむ → 楽しめる (potential, Group 1) + ようになりました.
❌ Not quite. できる IS the potential form of する already. できられる is double-potential and incorrect. Use できるようになりました directly.

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 Book 2 - Lesson 18: 将来、自分の会社を作ろうと思います
feedbackText: ❌ Not quite. 作る is Group 1: る→ろう. Remember: Group 2 verbs have い or え before る (見る, 食べる). 作る has う before る, so it's Group 1.
❌ Not quite. と思います = "I think I'll ~" (just now). 思っています = "I've been thinking about ~ing" (settled/ongoing). Different timing of the intention.
❌ Not quite. The answer is 働こうと思います. 働く → 働こう (volitional) + と思います.
❌ Not quite. The answer is ために. V-dictionary + ために for controllable goals.
❌ Not quite. 上手になる = "become skilled" — you can't directly will yourself to be skilled. It's a hoped-for outcome → ように. You control the studying (ために), not the becoming skilled (ように).

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 Book 2 - Lesson 2: まじめそうな人ですね
feedbackText: ❌ Not quite. The correct answer is が. In the pattern N1 は N2 が Adj, the particle が marks the body part (N2). Not を or は.
❌ Not quite. The correct answer uses が: あの人は目が大きいです. In this grammar pattern, the body part takes が, not を. Option C (あの人の目は) is grammatically possible but isn't the N1はN2が pattern being taught.

❌ Not quite. The correct answer is 背が高い人. When an イ-adjective clause modifies a noun, it goes directly before the noun — no の is needed. Option A adds unnecessary の, and option C uses a different structure (背の高い人 is also grammatically valid but not the pattern being practiced here).
❌ Not quite. The correct answer is メガネをかけている人. Japanese uses specific verbs: かける for glasses/sunglasses, 着る for upper body clothing, つける for accessories, かぶる for hats, はく for lower body.
❌ Not quite. The correct answer is 読んでいる (よんでいる). When modifying a noun, use the plain form: 読む → 読んで → 読んでいる. Not 読んでいます (polite form can't directly modify nouns).

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 Book 2 - Lesson 3: レストランで
feedbackText: ❌ Not quite. The correct answer is なので. ベジタリアン is treated as a ナ-adjective/noun, so it needs な before ので: ベジタリアンなので. Remember: ナ-adj/Noun + なので (not だので).
❌ Not quite. The correct answer is ので. Both ので and から give reasons, but ので is softer and more polite. けど means "but" — it's for contrast, not giving reasons.
❌ Not quite. The correct answer is 有名なので. The critical rule: ナ-adjectives and nouns use な before ので. NOT だので (option A) or のので (option C). This な replaces the usual だ.

❌ Not quite. The correct answer is よね. Since you already believe it closes at 10pm and want confirmation, よね ("It closes at 10, right?") is perfect. か is for when you genuinely don't know, and よ is for informing someone.
❌ Not quite. The correct answer is よね. You believe this dish contains shrimp and want to confirm: "This dish has shrimp in it, doesn't it?" This is the confirming-your-belief function of よね.

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 Book 2 - Lesson 4: しょうゆをつけないで食べてください
feedbackText: ❌ Not quite. The answer is なら. N なら picks up the keyword from the listener's request and responds with a tailored suggestion.
❌ Not quite. N なら picks up a keyword from what the listener said and responds with a recommendation. It doesn't give reasons (ので/から) or express contrast (が/けど).
❌ Not quite. The answer uses なら. The listener said おいしいすし, so you echo that keyword: おいしいすしなら. ので and から are for giving reasons, not recommendations.
❌ Not quite. The answer is かけて (て-form of かける). かけて食べる = "eat by putting/pouring on." The て-form describes how to eat.
❌ Not quite. The answer is 皮をむかないで. むく → むかない (ナイ-form) → むかないで ("without peeling"). Note: むかなくて is the て-form of the negative adjective — different grammar!

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 Book 2 - Lesson 5: 早く予約したほうがいいですよ
feedbackText: ❌ Not quite. The answer is 泳げる. 泳ぐ is a Group 1 verb: ぐ → げる. The u-sound changes to e-sound + る.
❌ Not quite. The particle changes from を to が. カニを食べる → カニが食べられる. This を→が change is specific to the potential form.
❌ Not quite. The answer is 見られる. 見る is Group 2: drop る → add られる. Note: 見える is a different verb meaning "to be visible" (intransitive), while 見られる is the potential "can see/watch."
❌ Not quite. The answer is ところ. どんなところ = "what kind of place." ところ is modified by どんな to ask about the characteristics of a location.
❌ Not quite. The answer uses な before ところ: きれいなところ. きれい is a ナ-adjective, so it requires な when modifying a noun. This is the same rule as with ので and のは.

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 Book 2 - Lesson 6: いろいろなところに行けて、よかったです
feedbackText: ❌ Not quite. The answer is つもりです. V-る (dictionary form) + つもりです = "I plan to V."
❌ Not quite. たい expresses desire (what you want), while つもり expresses a decided intention/plan (what you've committed to). They're different levels of certainty.
❌ Not quite. The answer is 行かないつもりです. For negative plans, use V-ない form + つもりです. 行くないつもり is incorrect conjugation. While つもりじゃない exists, V-ないつもり is the standard textbook pattern.
❌ Not quite. The answer is て. 行ける (potential) → 行けて (て-form). The て connects the ability to the emotion: "I could go, [so] I'm glad."
❌ Not quite. The answer uses 見られなくて: 見る → 見られる (potential) → 見られない (negative) → 見られなくて (なくて form). Option B skips the potential form. Option C has incorrect conjugation.

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 Book 2 - Lesson 7: 雨が降ったら、ホールでやります
feedbackText: ❌ Not quite. The answer is たら. 降る → 降った (past plain) + ら = 降ったら. Just add ら to the た form!
❌ Not quite. ナ-adjectives use だったら: 暇だ → 暇だった → 暇だったら. かったら is the イ-adjective pattern (悪い → 悪かったら). ならば is a different conditional form entirely.

❌ Not quite. The key difference is certainty: hypothetical conditions are uncertain (もし can be added), while fixed conditions will definitely happen. もし is a signal but not the defining factor — the real distinction is whether the event is guaranteed to occur.
❌ Not quite. The answer is 行われます. 行う → 行われる (passive: -u → -a + れる). This is the standard passive verb for official events and ceremonies.

❌ Not quite. The passive form puts the event as the subject, which is natural for announcements. It's not about politeness or formality per se — it's about information focus. The audience cares about the event, not the organizer.

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 Book 2 - Lesson 8: 屋台はどこかわかりますか？
feedbackText: ❌ Not quite. The answer is もう. もう + V-ました = "has [V] already?" This is the standard way to check if something has been completed.
❌ Not quite. まだです is the quickest, most natural reply. Option A uses まだ with positive (終わりました), which would mean the opposite. もうまだ is contradictory and grammatically incorrect.

❌ Not quite. まだ pairs with the negative て-form: まだ始まっていません. Option B uses もう (already) which contradicts "not yet." Option C uses ません without て, which means "won't start" rather than "hasn't started yet."
❌ Not quite. The answer is まだ. まだ + positive sentence = "still ~." It expresses that a state is continuing. もう would change the meaning entirely.
❌ Not quite. まだ + positive = "still ~" (continuing state). まだ + negative = "not yet" (hasn't happened). もう + positive = "already." The positive/negative pairing determines the meaning.

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 Book 2 - Lesson 9: 成人の日は、何をするんですか？
feedbackText: ❌ Not quite. The answer is んですか. Plain form + んですか asks for a detailed explanation.
❌ Not quite. んですか adds curiosity — it's asking for background/explanation, not just confirming a fact.
❌ Not quite. イ-adjectives connect directly: 高い + んですか. The な is only needed for ナ-adjectives and nouns.
❌ Not quite. ナA and Nouns need な before んですか. Verbs and イ-adj connect directly.
❌ Not quite. しか always pairs with a negative verb and expresses that the amount was less than expected.

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 - Lesson 1: 今の私
feedbackText:
✅ Answers
                    Grammar 1: Polite vs Plain Style
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        初はじめまして。田中たなかです。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: When meeting someone for the first time, especially in a formal situation like meeting your boss's family, you must use polite style (丁寧体). The です ending shows respect and is appropriate for this context.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        久ひさしぶり！元気げんき（nothing / だ is also OK）？
                    
                    
                        Explanation: With close friends, plain style (普通体) is natural. You can simply say 元気？ without any ending, or add だ (元気だ？) for slight emphasis. Using ですか？ with a close friend would sound distant!

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 2: Duration になります
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        仕事しごとを始はじめて３か月げつになります。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: になります follows the duration to express "it has become" that amount of time. This is the standard pattern for expressing elapsed time since an action began.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        日本語にほんごを勉強べんきょうして、どのぐらいになりますか？
                    
                    
                        Explanation: どのぐらい means "how long" or "how much" and is used to ask about duration. It's placed where the time period would go in the answer.

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 3: Point in Time
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        2023年ねんの４月がつに来きました。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: When specifying an exact date or month (like "April 2023"), you use the particle に. This is different from relative time words.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        去年きょねん来きました。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 去年きょねん means "last year." Relative time words like this do NOT take the particle に. So we say 去年きょねん来きました, not 去年きょねんに来きました.

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 4: V-ています
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        レストランで働はたらいています。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 働はたらく (to work) becomes 働はたらいて in te-form. Combined with います, it describes your current job/occupation.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        日本にほんでは、何なにをしていますか？
                    
                    
                        Explanation: する (to do) becomes して in te-form. しています means "do/are doing" and is the standard way to ask about someone's occupation or activities.

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 5: Te-form
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        読よんで
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 読よむ is a Group 1 verb ending in -む. Verbs ending in -ぶ, -む, -ぬ change to -んで. So 読よむ → 読よんで.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        食たべて
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 食たべる is a Group 2 verb (ends in -る with a vowel before it). Group 2 verbs simply drop -る and add -て. So 食たべる → 食たべて.

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 - Lesson 10: 日本語教室に参加したいんですが…
feedbackText:
✅ Answers
                    Grammar 1 & 2
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        茶道さどう教室きょうしつに参加さんかしてみたいんですが…。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 参加さんかします → 参加さんかして + みたいんですが. This expresses wanting to try something new for the first time, with the trailing "..." inviting a response.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        週しゅうに2回かいジムに行いきます。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: [Period]に[Number]回かい expresses frequency. 週しゅうに2回かい = twice a week.

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 3 & 4
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        よかったら、説明せつめいしましょうか？
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 説明せつめいします → 説明せつめいしましょうか？. This changes a statement into an offer to help. Adding よかったら makes it even more polite.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        すみません。「初心者しょしんしゃ」は、どういう意味いみですか？
                    
                    
                        Explanation: どういう意味いみですか asks "what does it mean?" This is essential for asking about unfamiliar vocabulary. (初心者しょしんしゃ = beginner)

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 5: 〜とき
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        大学生だいがくせいのとき、東京とうきょうに住すんでいました。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 大学生だいがくせい is a noun, so it takes の before とき. This describes when in the past the action happened.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        暇ひまなとき、映画えいがを見みます。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 暇ひま is a na-adjective, so it takes な before とき. 暇ひまなとき = "when (I'm) free."

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 - Lesson 11: 肉と野菜は私が買って行きます
feedbackText:
✅ Answers
                    Grammar 1 & 2
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        サラダを作つくって行いきます。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 作つくる → 作つくって + 行いきます. This means you will do the action (make a salad) first and then go somewhere with it.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        コーヒーとお茶ちゃ、どっちがいいですか？
                    
                    
                        Explanation: Since there are only two options (coffee and tea), use どっち (or どちら for formal). どれ is only used for 3 or more options.

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 3 & 4
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        ワインのほうがいいです。 (or ワインがいいです。)
                    
                    
                        Explanation: Nのほうがいいです expresses preference when choosing between two options. The shorter form Nがいいです also works and is commonly used.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        いつでもいいです。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: いつ (when) + でも = いつでも (anytime). Adding でも to a question word creates an indefinite meaning: "any-" (time/thing/where/one).

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 - Lesson 12: お弁当、おいしそうですね
feedbackText:
✅ Answers
                    Grammar 1 & 2
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        このカレー、辛からそうですね。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 辛からい → 辛からそう (remove い, add そう). This expresses your judgment based on how it looks.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        おいしそうなケーキ
                    
                    
                        Explanation: When modifying a noun, use 〜そうな. おいしい → おいしそうな + ケーキ = a delicious-looking cake.

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 3 & 4
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        このスープ、熱あつくて、おいしいです。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 熱あつい → 熱あつくて. The くて form connects adjectives and can also express a reason. The soup is delicious BECAUSE it's hot.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        このおすし、食たべてみてください。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 食たべる → 食たべて + みてください. V-てみてください is used to offer someone a chance to try something.

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 5 & 6
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        味噌汁みそしるには、豆腐とうふとわかめが入はいっています。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 入はいる (intransitive) + ています describes a state = "contains." Use N が 入はいっています to describe what something contains.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        部屋へや、寒さむくないですか？
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 寒さむい → 寒さむくないですか？ This negative question expresses concern for the other person's comfort. "Isn't it too cold?"

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 - Lesson 13: あと10分ぐらいで終わりそうです
feedbackText:
✅ AnswersGrammar 1 & 2
                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    プリンターが動うごかないんですが…。
                    Explanation: 動うごく (Group 1) → 動うごかない + んですが. The ナイ-form + んですが… is used to report problems and implicitly ask for help.
                
                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    あと30分ぷんぐらいで終おわりそうです。
                    Explanation: 終おわります → 終おわり + そうです. The で after the time indicates "in about 30 minutes."

✅ AnswersGrammar 3 & 4
                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    スキャンしたいときは、どうすればいいですか？
                    Explanation: する → したい + とき(は) = "when I want to do." This pattern asks for instructions.
                
                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    このボタンを押おすと、ドアが開あきます。
                    Explanation: V (dictionary form) + と = "if/when you V." The と shows automatic cause and effect.

✅ AnswersGrammar 5
                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    はい、ないです。
                    Explanation: The question "Haven't you been?" is correct (you haven't), so you say はい to confirm. はい + negative = "That's right, I haven't."
                
                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    いいえ、使つかったことがあります。
                    Explanation: The question "Haven't you used it?" is incorrect (you have), so you say いいえ to disagree. いいえ + positive = "Actually, I have."

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 - Lesson 14: 休みを取ってもいいでしょうか？
feedbackText:
✅ AnswersGrammar 1 & 2
                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    熱ねつがあって、会社かいしゃを休やすみました。
                    Explanation: ある → あって (V-て form). The て-form of verbs expresses cause/reason when connecting to a result.
                
                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    病院びょういんに行いきます。それで、少すこし遅刻ちこくします。
                    Explanation: それで connects two sentences where S1 is the reason for S2. "I'm going to the hospital. So, I'll be a little late."

✅ AnswersGrammar 3 & 4
                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    ちょっと休憩きゅうけいしてもいいですか？
                    Explanation: 休憩きゅうけいする → 休憩きゅうけいして + もいいですか = "May I take a break?" This is the standard way to ask permission.
                
                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    明日あした、休やすんでもいいでしょうか？
                    Explanation: 休やすむ → 休やすんで + もいいでしょうか. Using でしょうか makes it more polite—appropriate when asking your boss!

✅ AnswersGrammar 5 & 6
                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    病院びょういんに行いかなければなりません。
                    Explanation: 行いく → 行いかない → 行いかなければなりません. This expresses obligation: "I must go" / "I have to go."
                
                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    友達ともだちが来くるんです。
                    Explanation: V (dictionary form) + んです explains the situation. "A friend is coming." (That's why I need time off.)

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 - Lesson 15: 熱があってのどが痛いんです
feedbackText:
✅ Answers
                    Quiz Review
                
                
                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    頭あたまが痛いたいんです。
                    
                        Explanation: When explaining symptoms with イ-adjectives like 痛いたい (painful), you keep the い and add んです directly. This creates "The thing is, my head hurts" — a natural way to explain symptoms to a doctor.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    お風呂ふろに入はいらないでください。
                    
                        Explanation: To give a negative instruction "please don't," use V-ないでください. The ない-form of 入はいる (to enter) is 入はいらない, so the full expression is 入はいらないでください (please don't take a bath).
                    
                

                Continue Practice →

✅ Answers
                    Quiz Review
                
                
                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    食たべたあと、飲のんでください。
                    
                        Explanation: "After doing V" uses V-たあと. Since 食たべる's た-form is 食たべた, we get 食たべたあと (after eating). Remember: 前まえに uses dictionary form, あと uses た-form!
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    熱ねつを下さげる薬くすりです。
                    
                        Explanation: To describe what medicine does, use V(plain-form) + N. The verb 下さげる means "to lower/reduce," so 熱ねつを下さげる薬くすり = "medicine that lowers fever." Remember: modifiers come BEFORE the noun in Japanese!
                    
                

                Final Scenario →

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 - Lesson 16: 食べすぎないようにしています
feedbackText:
✅ Answers
                    Quiz Review
                
                
                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    入はいるといいですよ。
                    
                        Explanation: To give casual advice, use the dictionary form + といいですよ. The dictionary form of 入はいります is 入はいる. This creates a friendly suggestion: "It would be good if you take a warm bath."
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    食たべすぎて、お腹なかが痛いたいです。
                    
                        Explanation: V-すぎます expresses "too much." To connect it to a result (stomach hurts), use the て-form: すぎて. This pattern (V-すぎて、[result]) is very common for expressing negative consequences of excess.
                    
                

                Continue Practice →

✅ Answers
                    Quiz Review
                
                
                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    ジョギングしたり、ヨガをしたりしています。
                    
                        Explanation: V-たり、V-たり lists representative activities. Add り to the た-form: する → した → したり. This implies "I do things like jogging and yoga (among other activities)."
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    食たべるようにしています。
                    
                        Explanation: To express "I try to do X," use dictionary form + ようにしています. This shows conscious effort toward a healthy habit: "I make an effort to eat lots of vegetables."
                    
                

                Final Scenario →

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 - Lesson 17: 兄がくれたお守りです
feedbackText:
✅ Answers
                    Quiz Review
                
                
                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    ネパールから持もって来きた飾かざりです。
                    
                        Explanation: To describe something you brought, use V(た-form) + N. 持もって来きます becomes 持もって来きた in plain past form. This creates "a decoration (that) I brought from Nepal."
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    母ははにセーターをもらいました。
                    
                        Explanation: With もらいます, the giver is marked with に (or から). 母ははに = "from my mother." The pattern is [person]に[thing]をもらいます.
                    
                

                Continue Practice →

✅ Answers
                    Quiz Review
                
                
                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    兄あにがくれたお守まもりです。
                    
                        Explanation: With くれます, the giver is marked with が. This shows your brother as the subject who performed the action of giving to you. The た-form くれた modifies お守まもり (amulet).
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    父ちちが誕生日たんじょうびにくれました。
                    
                        Explanation: When talking about what someone gave you, use くれました. The pattern is [person]が[occasion]に[thing]をくれました. Here, the thing (watch) is implied from context.
                    
                

                Final Scenario →

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 - Lesson 18: 何かプレゼントをあげませんか？
feedbackText:
✅ Answers
                    Quiz Review
                
                
                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    結婚けっこんするそうですね。
                    
                        Explanation: To report hearsay about a future event, use the dictionary form + そうです. Since 結婚けっこんする is a verb in dictionary form, you simply add そうです after it.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    トアンさんにケーキをあげませんか？
                    
                        Explanation: With あげます, use に to mark the recipient and を to mark the thing being given. Pattern: [person]に[thing]をあげます.
                    
                

                Continue Practice →

✅ Answers
                    Quiz Review
                
                
                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    田中たなかさんは明日あした来くると言いっていました。
                    
                        Explanation: When directly quoting what someone said, use plain form + と言いっていました. The verb 来くる is already in dictionary (plain) form.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    佐藤さとうさんは来こないと思おもいます。
                    
                        Explanation: To express "I think (he) won't come," use the negative plain form (来こない) + と思おもいます. This is more natural than ～と思おもいません in Japanese.
                    
                

                Final Scenario →

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 - Lesson 2: ゲームをするのが好きです
feedbackText:
✅ Answers
                    Grammar 1: V-ることです
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        趣味しゅみは本ほんを読よむことです。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: Adding こと after the dictionary form (読よむ) turns "to read" into a noun phrase "reading." This is how we describe hobbies that involve actions.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        趣味しゅみは何なにですか？
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 何なに means "what." This is the standard way to ask about someone's hobby. The answer can be a noun (e.g., 旅行りょこうです) or a verb phrase (e.g., 音楽おんがくを聞きくことです).

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 2: V-るのが好すきです
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        音楽おんがくを聞きくのが好すきです。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: の nominalizes the verb phrase, turning "listen to music" into a noun that can be the subject of 好すきです. This is equivalent to saying "Listening to music is something I like."
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        料理りょうりをするのは、あまり好すきじゃないです。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: じゃない is the casual negative form. Combined with あまり (not really), this expresses a mild dislike. Note: じゃありません is also correct and more formal.

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 3: 人と・場所で
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        学校がっこうで日本語にほんごを勉強べんきょうします。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: The particle で marks the location where an action takes place. 学校がっこうで = "at school."
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        母ははと買かい物ものに行いきました。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: The particle と means "with" when referring to a person you do an activity together with. 母ははと = "with (my) mother."

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 4: V1-て、V2
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        朝あさごはんを食たべて、仕事しごとに行いきます。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 食たべる is a Group 2 verb (-る verb). Simply remove る and add て to get 食たべて. This connects the two actions in sequence.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        昨日きのう、映画えいがを見みて、買かい物ものをしました。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 見みる is also a Group 2 verb. 見みる → 見みて. The final verb (しました) carries the past tense for the whole sentence.

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 5: V-ています②
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        去年きょねんから、英語えいごを勉強べんきょうしています。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 勉強べんきょうする becomes 勉強べんきょうしています to indicate an ongoing activity. Combined with ～から (since), it shows something that started in the past and continues now.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        月つきに２回かい、ピアノを習ならっています。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 〜に〜回かい means "X times per ~" (月つきに２回かい = twice a month, 週しゅうに１回かい = once a week). This pattern is common when describing habitual activities.

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 - Lesson 3: 季節と天気
feedbackText:
✅ Answers
                    Grammar 1: なります
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        冬ふゆは寒さむくなります。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: For イ-adjectives, drop the final い and add く before なります. 寒さむい → 寒さむく + なります = "becomes cold."
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        部屋へやが静しずかになりました。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: For ナ-adjectives, simply add に before なります. 静しずか + に + なりました = "became quiet."

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 2: いちばん
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        夏なつがいちばん暑あついです。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: いちばん placed before the adjective creates the superlative "the most hot" = "the hottest."
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        好すきな季節きせつはいつですか？
                    
                    
                        Explanation: いつ means "when." This is the standard way to ask about someone's favorite season, time, or period.

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 3: イA-いのが好すき/苦手にがて
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        甘あまいのが好すきです。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: の nominalizes the adjective, turning "甘あまい" (sweet) into "sweet things" so it can be the subject of 好すきです.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        暑あついのが苦手にがてです。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 苦手にがて is a softer way to express dislike, meaning "not good with" rather than directly saying "dislike" (嫌きらい).

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 4: Adj + から
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        便利べんりですから。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: から follows です to give a reason. This sentence-final から is commonly used when answering "why?" questions.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        どうしてですか？
                    
                    
                        Explanation: どうして means "why." Other options include なぜ (more formal) or なんで (casual). どうしてですか is the most common way to ask for reasons.

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 5: S1から、S2
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        安やすいですから、好すきです。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: から connects the reason (安やすいです = it's cheap) to the result (好すきです = I like it). Note the comma after から for clarity.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        花はながきれいですから、春はるが好すきです。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: The context mentions spring flowers, so the logical result is liking 春はる (spring). Reason → Result structure.

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 - Lesson 4: 昨日はすごい雨でしたね
feedbackText:
✅ Answers
                    Grammar 1: Sね (Sympathy)
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        毎日まいにち、よく降ふりますね。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: Adding ね at the end invites the listener to agree with your observation about the weather. It creates a sense of shared experience.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        そうですね。 / 本当ほんとうですね。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: そうですね (Yes, that's right) and 本当ほんとうですね (That's true) are both natural ways to express agreement. The ね shows you share the same feeling.

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 2: Non-past & Past
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        昨日きのうは寒さむかったです。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: イ-adjectives form past tense by changing い to かった. 寒さむい → 寒さむかった + です = "was cold."
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        先週せんしゅうは雨あめでした。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: Nouns form past tense by changing です to でした. 雨あめです → 雨あめでした = "was rain/rainy."

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 3: V-ています③
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        今いま、雪ゆきが降ふっています。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 降ふる (to fall/rain/snow) becomes 降ふっています to describe current weather. 雪ゆきが降ふっています = "It is snowing."
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        今日きょうは晴はれていますね。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 晴はれる (to clear up/be sunny) becomes 晴はれています. This describes the current sunny state. Note: くもっています for cloudy works the same way.

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 4: 〜でしょう
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        明日あしたは暑あついでしょう。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: For イ-adjectives, simply add でしょう after the adjective (keep the い). 暑あつい + でしょう = "will probably be hot."
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        明日あしたは雨あめが降ふるでしょう。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: For verbs, use the dictionary form (plain non-past form) before でしょう. 降ふります → 降ふる + でしょう = "will probably fall/rain."

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 - Lesson 5: 私の町
feedbackText:
✅ Answers
                    Grammar 1: Connecting Descriptions
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        この町まちは静しずかで、いいですね。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 静しずか is a ナ-adjective, so we add で to connect it to another description. ナA + で = "is ~ and..."
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        このアパートは新あたらしくて、広ひろいです。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 新あたらしい is an イ-adjective. Drop い and add くて to connect. 新あたらしい → 新あたらしくて = "is new and..."

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 2: 〜けど (Contrast)
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        このアパートは小ちいさいけど、安やすいです。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: For イ-adjectives, simply add けど directly after the adjective. 小ちいさい + けど creates the contrast "small but..."
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        この辺へんは便利べんりだけど、うるさいです。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: For ナ-adjectives and nouns, add だけど. 便利べんり + だけど = "convenient but..." Note the contrast: positive (convenient) → negative (noisy).

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 3: V-ることができます
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        このプールで泳およぐことができます。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: こと turns the verb into a noun phrase. 泳およぐ (to swim) + こと = "swimming" or "the act of swimming." This becomes the subject of ができます.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        春はるは桜さくらを見みることができます。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: Use the dictionary form of the verb before こと. 見みます → 見みる (dictionary form). 見みる + ことができます = "can see."

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 - Lesson 6: 郵便局はどう行ったらいいですか？
feedbackText:
✅ Answers
                    Grammar 1: Nに行いきたいんですが…
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        病院びょういんに行いきたいんですが…。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: んですが makes the request softer by explaining your situation (you want to go somewhere) while implicitly asking for help. The trailing "…" invites the listener to respond.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        図書館としょかんは、どう行いったらいいですか？
                    
                    
                        Explanation: どう行いったらいいですか means "How should I go?" This is a more direct way to ask for directions compared to 〜んですが…

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 2: V-て、〜 Directions
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        まっすぐ行いって、右みぎに曲まがってください。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 行いく is a Group 1 verb ending in く. Te-form: く → いて. So 行いく → 行いって (irregular - one of the few exceptions!).
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        橋はしを渡わたって、左ひだりにあります。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 渡わたる is a Group 1 verb ending in る (not ru-verb!). Te-form: る → って. So 渡わたる → 渡わたって.

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 3: N1じゃなくて、N2
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        コーヒーじゃなくて、お茶ちゃです。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: じゃなくて connects the denial (not coffee) with the correction (tea). This is a polite way to correct a misunderstanding.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        1つ目めじゃなくて、3つ目めの角かどです。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: When giving directions, this pattern is very useful for correcting someone who misheard or misunderstood which landmark or turn you meant.

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 - Lesson 7: いっしょに出かける
feedbackText:
✅ Answers
                    Grammar 1: Nはどうですか？
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        駅えきはどうですか？
                    
                    
                        Explanation: Nはどうですか is the pattern for making suggestions. Here, 駅えき (station) is the place being suggested.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        7時半じはんはどうですか？
                    
                    
                        Explanation: The particle は marks the topic you're suggesting. 7時半じはんはどうですか asks the other person if 7:30 works for them.

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 2: Nでもいいですか？
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        4時じでもいいですか？
                    
                    
                        Explanation: でもいいですか is used when suggesting an alternative to what was originally proposed. The particle でも means "even" and makes it sound like "would even 4 o'clock be acceptable?"
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        コンビニでもいいですか？
                    
                    
                        Explanation: This pattern works for places too, not just times. You're asking if the convenience store would be an acceptable alternative meeting place.

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 3: Nで / V-て (Cause)
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        雨あめで、電車でんしゃが遅おくれました。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: For nouns indicating a cause, add で. 雨あめ (rain) + で = "because of rain." This is different from the で used for location or means!
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        道みちに迷まよって、遅おくれます。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 迷まよう is a Group 1 verb ending in う. Te-form: う → って. So 迷まよう → 迷まよって. The te-form here shows cause: "because I got lost..."

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 - Lesson 8: 野球、したことありますか？
feedbackText:
✅ Answers
                    Grammar 1 & 2
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        東京とうきょうタワー、もう行いきましたか？
                    
                    
                        Explanation: もう comes before the verb to ask "have you already...?" This implies Tokyo Tower is a popular spot that most visitors go to.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        寿司すしを食たべたことがありますか？
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 食たべる (Group 2) → 食たべた. Add ことがあります to ask about life experience: "Have you ever eaten...?"

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 3 & 4
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        ラーメンを食たべに行いきませんか？
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 食たべます → 食たべ (stem) + に行いきませんか = "Won't you go to eat ramen?" This is a polite invitation.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        映画えいがの前まえに、コーヒーを飲のみましょう。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: Nの前まえに means "before N." 映画えいがの前まえに = "before the movie."

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 5: V-たいんですが…
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        水みずを飲のみたいんですが…。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 飲のみます → 飲のみ (stem) + たいんですが… = "I'd like to drink (water)..." The trailing … invites the listener to respond or help.
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        写真しゃしんを撮とりたいんですが…。いいですか？
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 撮とります → 撮とり (stem) + たいんですが… The wish is followed by a polite request for permission.

results.title: いろどり Irodori A2 - Lesson 9: 読み方を教えてもらえませんか？
feedbackText:
✅ Answers
                    Grammar 1 & 2
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        YouTubeで日本語にほんごを勉強べんきょうしました。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: で indicates the means or method. YouTubeで = "through/using YouTube." This is different from で for place (学校で = at school).
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        韓国語かんこくごは発音はつおんが難むずかしいです。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: N1は marks the topic (韓国語かんこくご), and N2が marks the specific element being described (発音はつおん). "As for Korean, pronunciation is difficult."

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 3: Comparison
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        漢字かんじは難むずかしいです。でも、ひらがなは簡単かんたんです。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: Both 漢字かんじ and ひらがな take は because they are being contrasted. The second は implies "but as for hiragana..."
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        聞きくのは得意とくいです。でも、書かくのは苦手にがてです。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: When comparing skills, both use は. の nominalizes the verbs (聞きくの = listening, 書かくの = writing), and は marks the contrast.

✅ Answers
                    Grammar 4 & 5
                

                
                    ✓ Question 1 Answer
                    
                        辞書じしょを貸かしてもらえませんか？
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 貸かします → 貸かして + もらえませんか. This is a polite way to request something, literally "Could I receive the favor of you lending...?"
                    
                

                
                    ✓ Question 2 Answer
                    
                        この機械きかいの使つかい方かたがわかりません。
                    
                    
                        Explanation: 使つかいます → 使つかい (masu-stem) + 方かた = 使つかい方かた (how to use). V-方かた creates a noun meaning "the way of doing V."

results.title: Lesson 10 — Existence & Location | Minna no Nihongo N5
feedbackText: 🎉Correct!✅ A cat is animate (can move by itself), so use います. 猫が います = There's a cat. あります is for inanimate things that can't move on their own.
😅Not quite!✅ Cherry trees (桜) are plants — they can't move by themselves, so use あります even though they're alive! Plants always use あります in Japanese.
😅Not quite! Answer: に ～ がPlace に marks the location; 何が uses が because the thing is unknown (question word). Remember: after interrogatives, always use が, never は!
😅Not quite!✅ Grammar 3: N は Place に います. 田中さん (known person) は marks the topic; 事務所 (office) に marks the location of existence. The person is known → は. The location → に.
😅Not quite! Answer: の ～ にテーブルの 下に = "at the under of the table" = under the table. の connects the reference object to the position word; に marks the location of existence.

results.title: Lesson 11 — Counting & Quantifiers | Minna no Nihongo N5
feedbackText: 😅Not quite!✅ 5人 = ごにん. From 3 onward, the people counter is regular: さんにん, よにん, ごにん... Only 1人（ひとり）and 2人（ふたり）are irregular.
😅Not quite! Answer: 3枚（さんまい）Stamps are thin, flat objects → use 枚（まい）. 切手を 3枚 買いました. Remember: 枚 is for paper, shirts, plates, tickets — anything thin and flat!
😅Not quite! Answer: どのくらいどのくらい asks "how long" for durations. ここから 駅まで どのくらい かかりますか = How long does it take from here to the station?
🎉Correct!✅ ぐらい makes the number approximate. 20分ぐらい = "about 20 minutes." Without ぐらい, 20分 means exactly 20 minutes. Use ぐらい when you're not being precise.
😅Not quite! Answer: にに marks the time frame for frequency: 1週間に 2回 = twice PER week. This に means "per" — the period within which the action happens a certain number of times.

results.title: Lesson 12 — Past Adjectives & Comparisons | Minna no Nihongo N5
feedbackText: 😅Not quite! Answer: 楽しかったですい-adj past: drop い → add かった. 楽しい → 楽しかった. Remember: the い always drops first, then add the past ending!
🎉Correct!✅ いい is always irregular! いい → よかった (past aff.) → よくなかった (past neg.). NEVER いかった or いくなかった. Always change いい to よ- first, then conjugate normally.
😅Not quite! Answer: よりより means "than." 東京は 大阪より 大きいです = Tokyo is bigger THAN Osaka. より comes after the thing being compared against (大阪).
😅Not quite! Answer: の ほうがの ほうが indicates your choice when comparing two things. すしの ほうが 好きです = Sushi (is the side that) I like more. ほう literally means "direction/side."
😅Not quite! Answer: いちばんいちばん = "number one / the most." スポーツで 何が いちばん おもしろいですか = Among sports, what is THE MOST interesting?

results.title: Lesson 13 — Desires & Purpose | Minna no Nihongo N5
feedbackText: 😅Not quite! Answer: がThe desired object takes が with ほしい, just like 好き, 上手, わかる in L9. This is the "state/feeling" pattern — things you experience/feel toward use が, not を.

🎉Correct!✅ ほしいですか is too direct for offering — it feels like interrogating someone about their desires. Instead use いかがですか (how about ~?) or 飲みませんか (won't you drink ~?) for polite offers. This is an important cultural note from the textbook!
😅Not quite! Answer: 行きたい行きます → ます-form = 行き → 行き + たい = 行きたいです. Remove ます, then add たい!
🎉Correct!✅ たい conjugates like an い-adjective! Past negative: たい → たくなかった. 飲みたくなかったです = didn't want to drink. Option A uses じゃ ありませんでした which is for な-adjectives and nouns, NOT い-adjectives!
😅Not quite! Answer: にに marks the PURPOSE of going. 買い (ます-form of 買います) + に 行きます = go TO buy. The ます-form (買い) connects to に to show why you're going.

results.title: Lesson 14 — て-form Revolution | Minna no Nihongo N5
feedbackText: 😅Not quite! Answer: 飲んで（のんで）Rule (2): み, び, に → んで. 飲みます: み → んで = 飲んで. Remember the song: "mi-bi-ni NDE"!
😅Not quite! Answer: 書いて（かいて）Rule (3): き → いて. 書きます: き → いて = 書いて. If it were ぎ (voiced), it would become いで instead.
🎉Correct!✅ Group II verbs simply add て to the ます-form. 食べます → 食べ + て = 食べて. No sound changes needed! Group II is the simplest — no sub-rules to remember.
😅Not quite! Answer: 待って（まって）Rule (1): い, ち, り → って. 待ちます: ち → って = 待って. 待って ください = Please wait.
😅Not quite! Answer: 貸して（かして）Rule (4): し → して. 貸します: し → して = 貸して. 傘を 貸して ください = Please lend me your umbrella.

results.title: Lesson 15 — Permission, Prohibition & States | Minna no Nihongo N5
feedbackText: 😅Not quite! Answer: ても いいですかV て-form + も いいですか = asking permission. 座ります → 座って (り→って) + も いいですか = May I sit?

😅Not quite!✅ Prohibition uses ては いけません. The は marks the action as the topic being declared "not acceptable." Option B (ても) would be the permission pattern — wrong context! Remember: も = permission (even if), は = prohibition (as for).

🎉Correct!✅ The negative of 知って います is 知りません — NOT 知って いません! This is the #1 exception in N5. 知る is an instantaneous verb; 知って います describes the RESULTING STATE of knowing. The negative returns to the simple ません form.
😅Not quite! Answer: で います住みます (み→んで) = 住んで. Then + います for state: 住んで います = live (in). The で here is part of the て-form conjugation (み→んで), NOT the particle で!
😅Not quite!✅ The key clue is 今 (now). When 今 is present, て います means an action is in progress RIGHT NOW. 今 電話を かけて います = is making a phone call right now. Without 今, it might be habitual.

results.title: Lesson 16 — Connecting & Describing | Minna no Nihongo N5
feedbackText: 😅Not quite! Answer: 安くてい-adjective linking: 安い → drop い, add くて = 安くて. This connects to the next adjective (いいです) to describe the same bag.
😅Not quite! Answer: でな-adjective linking: 親切 + で = 親切で. な-adjectives and nouns both use で to link — same pattern as the copula (です → でした → で).
😅Not quite! Answer: からてから = "after doing." 浴びてから 食べます = After showering, I eat. から marks the shower as a precondition/preparatory step before breakfast.
🎉Correct!✅ In a て-form chain, ONLY the last verb carries tense. 大阪へ 行って、お好み焼きを 食べて、帰りました — 帰りました (past) sets the entire chain to past tense. The て-form verbs are tense-neutral connectors.
😅Not quite! Answer: は ～ がは sets the big topic (Tokyo). が marks the specific aspect being described (people). 東京は 人が 多いです = As for Tokyo, people are numerous.

results.title: Lesson 17 — ない-form & Obligations | Minna no Nihongo N5
feedbackText: 😅Not quite! Answer: 飲まない（のまない）Group I: い-column → あ-column. み → ま + ない = 飲まない. The み sound shifts to its あ-column partner ま, then add ない.
😅Not quite! Answer: 買わない（かわない）Exception! When ます-form ends in い, it becomes わない (NOT あない). 買います: い → わ + ない = 買わない.

🎉Correct!✅ 来ます → 来ない. The reading changes from き to こ! This is a Group III irregular — the kanji 来 keeps the same character but its pronunciation shifts. This catches many learners off guard. 来ない（こない）is the correct form.
😅Not quite! Answer: ないで くださいGroup II: 忘れます → 忘れ + ない = 忘れない + で ください = 忘れないで ください. Group II is simple — just add ないで ください to the stem!
😅Not quite! Answer: 吸わないでException! 吸います: い → わ + ない = 吸わない + で = 吸わないで ください. Remember the い→わ exception!

results.title: Lesson 18 — Dictionary Form & Abilities | Minna no Nihongo N5
feedbackText: 😅Not quite! Answer: 飲む（のむ）Group I: い-column → う-column. み → む. 飲みます → 飲む. The み sound shifts to its う-column partner む.
😅Not quite! Answer: 話す（はなす）Group I: し → す. 話します → 話す. し shifts to its う-column partner す.
🎉Correct!✅ 来ます → 来る. The reading changes from き to く! Three readings for 来: 来（き）ます, 来（こ）ない, 来（く）る. This is the most irregular verb in Japanese — always memorize all three forms.
😅Not quite! Answer: ぐ泳ぎます: ぎ → ぐ (dictionary form). 泳ぐ ことが できます = can swim. The dictionary form (泳ぐ) + こと turns it into a noun phrase meaning "the act of swimming."

😅Not quite!✅ With できます, the ability takes が, NOT を. 日本語が できます = can do Japanese. This follows the same pattern as が with 好き (L9), 欲しい (L13), and わかります (L9) — abilities, feelings, and comprehension all use が!

results.title: Lesson 19 — た-form, Experience & Change | Minna no Nihongo N5
feedbackText: 😅Not quite! Answer: 読んだ読んで → 読んだ. で → だ! When the て-form ends in で, the た-form ends in だ. It's that simple.
😅Not quite! Answer: 食べた食べて → 食べた. て → た! Group II is always the simplest — just swap the ending.
😅Not quite! Answer: 遊んだ遊んで → 遊んだ. で → だ! The ん stays, で becomes だ.
😅Not quite! Answer: た ことが あります(か)行って → 行った (て→た). 行った ことが あります = have been (to Japan). This asks about life experience — "at any point, have you ever gone?"

🎉Correct!✅ When you mention a SPECIFIC time (去年 = last year), use the simple past ました, NOT ことがあります. ことがあります is for general life experience without specifying when. 去年 日本へ 行きました = I went to Japan last year (specific event).

results.title: Lesson 2 — これ / それ / あれ | Minna no Nihongo N5
feedbackText:
😅

results.title: Lesson 20 — Plain Style & Casual Speech | Minna no Nihongo N5
feedbackText: 😅Not quite! Answer: 食べなかった食べない (ない-form) → 食べなかった (ない → なかった). The plain negative past works just like an い-adjective past: ない → なかった, the same way 高い → 高かった.
😅Not quite!✅ な-adjective plain affirmative: です → だ. 好きです → 好きだ. However, note that in QUESTIONS, だ is dropped: 好き？ (NOT 好きだ？). The だ only appears in statements.
😅Not quite! Answer: たいたいです → たい. Since たい conjugates like an い-adjective, its plain form simply drops です: 登りたいです → 登りたい. Just like 忙しいです → 忙しい.
🎉Correct!✅ い-adjectives are already plain without です — just drop it! おいしいです → おいしい. There is NO だ added to い-adjectives. Only な-adjectives and nouns use だ. Saying おいしいだ is a common learner mistake!

🎉Correct!✅ に (location/time marker) cannot be dropped because the meaning would become unclear. を, へ, and は can be dropped when meaning is clear from context. But で, に, から, まで, and と carry essential meaning that can't be inferred.

results.title: Lesson 21 — Think, Say & Confirm | Minna no Nihongo N5
feedbackText: 😅Not quite! Answer: いとい-adjective plain form is just the adjective itself: 忙しい. Then add と 思います. 忙しいと 思います = I think (he) is busy. い-adjectives need no だ — they're already plain as-is!

😅Not quite!✅ Japanese negates the THOUGHT, not 思います. 来ないと 思います = I think he won't come. This is the opposite of English ("I don't think he'll come"). The negation goes BEFORE と, inside the quoted thought.
😅Not quite! Answer: だとな-adjective plain affirmative: 静か + だ (L20). Before と: 静かだと 思います. Remember: な-adj plain form needs だ in statements, unlike い-adj which stands alone!
😅Not quite! Answer: くと行きます → 行く (dictionary form = plain affirmative). 行くと 言いました = said she would go. Indirect speech always uses plain form before と.

😅Not quite!✅ The LISTENER (person you tell something to) is marked with に. 母に ～と 言いました = I told my mother. 母が would mean "my mother said" — が marks the speaker/subject. に marks the recipient of speech.

results.title: Lesson 22 — Noun Modification & Relative Clauses | Minna no Nihongo N5
feedbackText: 😅Not quite!✅ In Japanese, the modifying clause ALWAYS comes BEFORE the noun. 日本語を 話す 人 = a Japanese-speaking person / a person who speaks Japanese. This is the opposite of English word order, where the relative clause ("who speaks Japanese") comes after the noun.
😅Not quite! Answer: 買った買います → 買った (た-form = plain past). きのう 買った 本 = the book I bought yesterday. The た-form clause acts as an adjective for 本!
😅Not quite! Answer: なな-adjective + noun: 親切な 人. This is the same rule as L8! な-adjectives always use な when directly modifying a noun, whether the description is simple or part of a longer clause.

😅Not quite! Answer: 映画はきのう 見た = modifier, 映画 = modified noun, は = topic marker. きのう 見た 映画は おもしろかったです = The movie I watched yesterday was interesting. The modified noun (映画) takes は because it's the sentence topic.

🎉Correct!✅ 好きです requires が (L9). ミラーさんが 作った ケーキが 好きです = I like the cake that Miller baked. The first が marks the subject inside the modifying clause (Miller), the second が marks what you like (the cake). Two が's, two different functions!

results.title: Lesson 23 — When & If (とき・と) | Minna no Nihongo N5
feedbackText: 😅Not quite! Answer: なな-adjective before とき (a noun) uses な — the same rule as L8/L22 noun modification. 暇な とき = free time / when free. This is identical to 暇な 人 (a free person).
😅Not quite! Answer: のNoun before とき uses の — same as L2/L22 noun modification. 子どもの とき = childhood time. NOT 子どもだった とき — the main clause (遊びました) carries the past tense.
😅Not quite! Answer: ときわからない (ない-form) + とき = when you don't understand. ない-form connects directly to とき, just like it directly modifies nouns in L22.
😅Not quite!✅ Dictionary form (行く) + とき = BEFORE completing the action. You met Tanaka on the way to the office, before arriving. た-form (行った) would mean after arriving at the office.
😅Not quite! Answer: た来た とき = after arriving. The bag was bought IN Japan (after coming). If it were 来る とき, the dictionary would have been bought BEFORE arriving in Japan (on the way).

results.title: Lesson 24 — Giving & Receiving Actions | Minna no Nihongo N5
feedbackText: 😅Not quite! Answer: あげましたI do for someone = て あげます. I'm the doer, Tanaka is the recipient (に). 教えて あげました = I taught him (as a favor). The direction is outward: me → Tanaka.
😅Not quite! Answer: もらいましたI receive the benefit = て もらいます. The speaker received help FROM the friend (友達に). 手伝って もらいました = I had my friend help me / I received the favor of being helped.
😅Not quite! Answer: くれましたSomeone does for ME = て くれます. My mother (母が) is the doer who gives the action to me. 作って くれました = she kindly made it for me. くれます focuses on the giver's kind action.

🎉Correct!✅ Focus on Yamada's KINDNESS = て くれます. 山田さんが (subject = doer) 写真を 見せて くれました = Yamada kindly showed me the photos. てくれます puts the spotlight on the other person's generous action. てもらいます would focus on YOUR receiving instead.
😅Not quite! Answer: くれますFriend (が = subject/doer) kindly takes me → て くれます. The friend's kind action benefits me. 連れて 行って くれます = (my friend) kindly takes me. くれます focuses on THEIR generosity.

results.title: Lesson 25 — たら & ても — The Grand Finale! | Minna no Nihongo N5
feedbackText: 😅Not quite! Answer: かったら安い → past: 安かった + ら = 安かったら (if it's cheap). い-adjective たら: drop い, add かったら. The main clause 買います (will buy) shows intention — this is OK with たら but NOT with と!
😅Not quite! Answer: だったら暇 (な-adj) → past: 暇だった + ら = 暇だったら (if you're free). な-adjectives and nouns both use だったら. The main clause has a request (てください) — only possible with たら, not と!
😅Not quite! Answer: たら降る → た-form: 降った + ら = 降ったら (if it rains). Plain past form + ら = たら. This is the most basic and common verb pattern for たら.
😅Not quite! Answer: くても高い → て-form: 高くて + も = 高くても (even if expensive). い-adj ても: drop い, add くても. Normally expensive = don't buy, but ても says you'll buy ANYWAY!
😅Not quite! Answer: ても降る → て-form: 降って + も = 降っても (even if it rains). V ても: て-form + も. Compare: 降ったら 行きません (if it rains, I won't go) vs 降っても 行きます (even if it rains, I WILL go)!

results.title: Lesson 3 — ここ / そこ / あそこ | Minna no Nihongo N5
feedbackText: 🎉Correct!✅ The restroom is far from BOTH you and your friend, so あそこ (over there) is correct. Since you're sharing the same space, ここ = your shared area, and あそこ = far away.
🎉Correct!✅ こちらです is much more polite and appropriate for hotel/service staff. ここです is understandable but too casual for professional settings.
😅Not quite! The answer is: 1階（いっかい）The place (1階 = first floor) goes after は. This is the N は place です pattern.
😅Not quite!✅ When a person + は + place + です, it means that person IS at that place. 田中さんは 食堂です = "Mr. Tanaka is in the cafeteria."
😅Not quite! The answer is: どこ / どちらBoth どこ and どちら work here. どちら is more polite and also implies direction — perfect for asking about the location of something in a building.

results.title: Lesson 4 — Time & Verbs | Minna no Nihongo N5
feedbackText: 😅Not quite!✅ 4時 is read as よじ — this is an irregular reading. しじ and よんじ are both incorrect. Remember the three tricky hours: 4時 = よじ, 7時 = しちじ, 9時 = くじ.
😅Not quite! The answer is: 何時（なんじ）何時 (なんじ) means "what time." Combined with 今 (いま = now), it gives you the essential phrase 今 何時ですか。
🎉Correct!✅ "Didn't study yesterday" needs the past negative form: 勉強しませんでした. きのう signals past tense, and "didn't" signals negative → past + negative = ませんでした.
😅Not quite! The answer is: 起きます（おきます）Echo the verb from the question! 起きます (おきます) = "I get up." The question asked 何時に 起きますか, so you reply with the same verb.
🎉Correct!✅ きのう (yesterday) is a relative time word, so it does NOT take に. Only specific/numerical times like 6時, 7月2日 require に.

results.title: Lesson 5 — Going & Coming | Minna no Nihongo N5
feedbackText: 🎉Correct!✅ The particle へ is always pronounced え (e) when used as a particle, just like は is pronounced わ (wa). This is one of the most important pronunciation rules in Japanese!
😅Not quite! Answer: へへ marks the destination/direction of movement. 日本へ 来ました = I came TO Japan. Remember: へ is read as え!
🎉Correct!✅ The pattern requires BOTH: interrogative + も AND a negative verb. だれも 来ませんでした = nobody came. Option A uses an affirmative verb (wrong). Option B is missing も (wrong).
😅Not quite! Answer: も + ませんInterrogative + も + negative verb = total negation. どこ[へ]も 行きません = not going anywhere. Both どこも and どこへも are acceptable!
😅Not quite! Answer: でで marks the means of transport. バスで 行きます = go BY bus. Think of で as "by means of."

results.title: Lesson 6 — N を V & Invitations | Minna no Nihongo N5
feedbackText: 😅Not quite! Answer: をを marks the direct object — the thing being acted upon. 新聞を 読みます = I read a newspaper. The を is pronounced お but written を.
😅Not quite!✅ サッカーを します = play soccer. The particle を marks the activity noun as the object of します. で marks transport or place, not objects. に marks direction or time.
😅Not quite! Answer: 何（なに）何 is read as なに before を (a particle, not a t/d/n sound). なにを しましたか = What did you do?
😅Not quite!✅ 何 is read as なん before d/t/n-row sounds. です starts with "de" (d-row), so it's なんですか. Before particles like を, it's なにを.
😅Not quite! Answer: でで marks the place where an action happens. 食堂で 昼ごはんを 食べます = I eat lunch AT the cafeteria. Notice both で (place) and を (object) in one sentence!

results.title: Lesson 7 — Giving & Receiving | Minna no Nihongo N5
feedbackText: 😅Not quite! Answer: でで marks the tool/means used to do something. はしで 食べます = eat WITH chopsticks. This is the third use of で (after transport L5 and location L6).
😅Not quite! Answer: でで here means "in [language]" — the language is the means of expression. 日本語で 何ですか = What is it IN Japanese?
😅Not quite! Answer: に ～ を田中さんに = TO Mr. Tanaka (recipient marked by に), 本を = a book (object marked by を). Full: 田中さんに 本を あげました。
🎉Correct!✅ The bank (銀行) is an organization, so you MUST use から, not に. 銀行から お金を 借りました = I borrowed money FROM the bank. Remember: organizations → から only!
😅Not quite! Answer: もうもう = already/yet. もう 昼ごはんを 食べましたか = Have you eaten lunch yet? It asks whether the action has been completed.

results.title: Lesson 8 — Adjectives | Minna no Nihongo N5
feedbackText: 😅Not quite!✅ おもしろい is an い-adjective. To negate: drop い → add くない. おもしろくないです. You CANNOT use じゃ ありません with い-adjectives — that's only for な-adjectives and nouns!
🎉Correct!✅ いい (good) is IRREGULAR! Its negative is よくないです, NOT いくないです. This is because いい originally comes from よい. All conjugated forms use よ-: よくない, よかった, etc.
😅Not quite! Answer: な有名 is a な-adjective, so it needs な before the noun. 有名な 映画 = a famous movie. Remember: 有名 ends in い but it's still a な-adjective — don't be tricked!
🎉Correct!✅ おもしろい is an い-adjective, so it keeps い before a noun: おもしろい 本. You do NOT add な after い-adjectives — な is only for な-adjectives.
😅Not quite! Answer: がが connects two contrasting statements about the same subject. 静かですが、古いです = quiet BUT old. The first clause is positive, the second negative — a classic が contrast.

results.title: Lesson 9 — Likes, Abilities & Reasons | Minna no Nihongo N5
feedbackText: 😅Not quite! Answer: がわかります (understand) uses が, not を. This is because understanding is a STATE you experience, not an ACTION you perform on something. 日本語が わかります = I understand Japanese.
🎉Correct!✅ 好きです (like) uses が, not を. 好き is a な-adjective describing your feeling TOWARD something — so the object uses が. イタリア料理が 好きです = I like Italian food.
😅Not quite! Answer: ぜんぜんぜんぜん (not at all) is the strongest negative adverb. ぜんぜん ありません = don't have ANY at all. Remember: ぜんぜん MUST be used with a negative verb!
😅Not quite!✅ すこし (a little) is used with affirmative sentences. ぜんぜん is only used with NEGATIVE sentences. ぜんぜん わかります is grammatically wrong — you need ぜんぜん わかりません.
😅Not quite! Answer: からから links the reason (don't like meat) to the result (don't eat meat). 好きじゃ ありませんから、食べません = Because I don't like it, I don't eat it.
